questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some blankets are pillows. All pillows are books. Conclusions: I. Some books are blankets. II. Some blankets are pillows. III. No book is a pillow. IV. Some blankets are not books.

choices.b: I and either II or III follow
choices.c: Either I or II and III follow
choices.d: Either I or TV and II
choices.e: Either I or II or III follows

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No girl is an Indian. No American is an Indian. Conclusions: I. Some girls are Americans. II. Some Americans are not Indians. III. All girls are Americans. IV. No girls are Americans.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All classes are glasses. All brasses are glasses. Conclusions: I. Some classes are brasses. II. Some brasses are glasses. III. Some brasses are classes. IV. Some classes are above.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No Indian is an Asian. Some Americans are Indians. Conclusions: I. Some Indians are not Asians. II. Some Asians are not Americans. III. Some Americans are not Asians. IV. All Americans are Asians.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Only red are blues. All reds are yellows. Conclusions: I. Some blues are yellows. II. No blues are yellows. III. Some yellows are reds. IV. Some yellows are not reds.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Only I follows Either I or II follows All and III follow Some throngs are longs. Conclusions: I. Some songs are longs. II. No songs are throngs. III. Only longs are songs. IV. Only songs are longs.

choices.c: Either I and either III or IV follow
choices.d: Either I or either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some books are helmets. Only copies are helmets. Conclusions: I. Some books are copies. II. All helmets are copies. III. All copies are helmets. IV. Some copies are not helmets.

choices.b: II and either III or IV follow
choices.c: I, II and either III or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some states are capitals. No brass capitals. Conclusions: I. Some states are not pens. II. All states are pens. III. Some pens are not states. IV. All pens are not states.

choices.e: Either III or IV and I follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: No pen is a pencil. All pencils are streets. Conclusions: I. No pen is a street. II. No street is a pen. III. Some streets are not pens. IV. Some pens are not streets.
